# F025. Fundamental Delay-Aware LightGBM Model

## Positioning

This candidate is about enforcing the annoying but necessary truth that fundamentals arrive late and sometimes revise. The model is only useful if the lag is explicit and the score stays honest about what was actually known at the time.

## Reference Basis

- [LightGBM: A Highly Efficient Gradient Boosting Decision Tree](../../references/papers/lightgbm_neurips_2017.pdf) - the tabular score engine
- [Multivariate Bayesian Structural Time Series Model](../../references/papers/1801.03222_multivariate_bsts.pdf) - latent trend and lag-aware state framing
- [View fusion vis-à-vis a Bayesian interpretation of Black-Litterman for portfolio allocation](../../references/papers/2301.13594_black_litterman_fusion.pdf) - posterior fusion when fundamentals and technicals overlap

## Repo Anchors

### Operational Guardrails

Shared safety and execution rails used by every candidate before a signal can touch capital.
- [src/risk/manager.py](../../../src/risk/manager.py) - pre-trade risk gate and position haircut
- [src/brokers/paper.py](../../../src/brokers/paper.py) - paper-safe broker adapter
- [src/monitoring/health.py](../../../src/monitoring/health.py) - freshness, heartbeat, and kill-switch checks
- [src/backtest/engine.py](../../../src/backtest/engine.py) - deterministic offline backtest runner
- [src/backtest/matching_engine.py](../../../src/backtest/matching_engine.py) - queue-aware synthetic fill model
- [scripts/monitor_health.py](../../../scripts/monitor_health.py) - runtime health loop and stale-data monitor

### Fundamental Stack

Point-in-time fundamentals and macro-aware feature joins for slower rebalance cycles.
- [src/ingestion/loader.py](../../../src/ingestion/loader.py) - fundamental and macro data loader
- [src/features/builder.py](../../../src/features/builder.py) - feature joins and lag alignment
- [src/models/lgbm.py](../../../src/models/lgbm.py) - tabular score model for the hybrid stack

### Cross-Sectional Ranker

The executable ranking path that turns scores into a tradable long/flat or top-k book.
- [src/models/lgbm.py](../../../src/models/lgbm.py) - LightGBM scoring model
- [src/features/builder.py](../../../src/features/builder.py) - feature matrix construction for the ranker

## Data / Feature Bundle

- Keep the fundamental feed point-in-time and apply the actual publication lag before any join.
- Blend technicals and fundamentals only after the lagged data is aligned in `src/features/builder.py`.
- Use the same cross-sectional universe as F012 so the lag effect can be isolated.

## Execution Path

- Rebalance only when the fundamentals are legitimately available.
- Let the LightGBM model score the delayed fundamental snapshot rather than the raw reporting date.
- Use the risk manager to ensure the slower cadence does not accidentally expand turnover.

## Risk Controls

- Treat any report-date mismatch as a hard failure.
- Reject the model if the lag logic becomes too complex to audit.
- Do not allow late revisions to leak into historical training labels.

## Validation Gates

- Check explicitly for lookahead around announcements and filing dates.
- Compare the delayed model to F012 on the same assets and same rebalance cadence.
- Measure whether the lagged fundamentals still add value after costs.

## Archive Triggers

- Archive if the delay logic is too brittle for production use.
- Archive if the lag erases the edge after transaction costs.
- Archive if the report-date handling is not maintainable.

## Next Step

Use this as the canonical test of whether fundamental delay handling is robust enough for live use.
